topic: Best course structure for the Max Inhouse info business scaling transcript

conversation.content: 6 modules following pipeline dependency graph. KPIs at position 5. One constraint exercise per module. Compounding = action velocity.
4 self-contained modules. Lead with outreach not identity. Profile moved to end. KPI benchmarks inline. Content strategy separate track.
Warns: sequence trap (loop not pipeline), KPIs without baselines, Loom requires working offer. Design backwards from KPI dashboard.
Build/Fill/Convert/Run the Machine. Output-based exercises. Content strategy placement question.
Open with live reps (10 DMs first). Profile last as refinement. KPIs second. Cut fulfillment/team. Backwards from close.
Live rep opening is diagnostic tool. Accept loop vs pipeline critique. Minimum viable offer as prerequisite. Content last. Parallel theory + dashboard tracks.
Live rep best idea but need functional profile first (30 min). Offer = 1 paragraph gate. Loop is phase 2 problem. Content + team = separate.
Live rep breaks without defined offer. Profile = recurring checkpoint not module. Show benchmarks after real data. Design against completes-course-never-closes.
Live rep needs training wheels. Minimum viable offer as module 1 deliverable. Emotional checkpoints. Content last.
Pressure test: send DMs, log what breaks, course teaches why. Instruction follows failure. Content always last.
State 0 gate (profile + offer + target persona). Failure-mode modules: nobody replies / replies no calls / calls no closes / closes delivery breaks. Dashboard blank template filled with student data.
3 phases: Day 1 setup, Week 1 DMs, fix what broke. Content + team separate. Emotional checkpoints = one line, not section.
Unclear offer contaminates rep data. 2-hour offer+ICP gate. Gate needs a gate. Students can't self-diagnose.
Gated deliverables per module. Two tracks (builders + testers) converging at KPI dashboard.
Students already have failure data. Course should be audit + diagnostic. KPI dashboard as spine. Then self-corrected: audit only works after students learn to read data. Gate > Reps > Audit.
After State 0, course stops being sequential. Dashboard becomes nav system. Diagnostic tree with four failure-mode nodes. Question: independent modules or intake diagnostic?
Two-track entry (Track A: never sent DMs, Track B: has history). Both converge at failure modules. Dashboard = first thing students see.
Students can't self-report what broke. Gate needs enforcing mechanism. Delivery context (live vs async) matters. Two unanswered questions block final structure.
State 0 outputs three locked assets. Track A = gated deliverables. Track B = diagnostic first. Converge at KPI dashboard at end.
Self-corrected: audit only works after reps. KPI dashboard = last thing unlocked. Three stages: Gate > Reps with structured logging > Audit with legible data.
Gate (Week 0) > Reps with Structured Logging (Weeks 1-2) > Diagnostic Tree (Week 3+). Content + team separate. Emotional checkpoints embedded. Dashboard diagnoses, not students.
Phase 0: Gate. Phase 1: Reps (Weeks 1-2). Phase 2: Audit (Week 3). Phase 3: Delivery (Week 4). 4 phases, 4 weeks. Ships fast.
Gate (Module 0). Track split (Track A: first reps, Track B: audit existing). Converge at logging. Reps (Modules 2-3). Audit (Module 4). Content + team out.
Gate > Track A/B > Logged Data Sprint > Optimization Layer. Content + team = different course. Action precedes theory, data precedes diagnosis.
Module 0: Gate (pass/fail). Module 1: Reps with logging. Module 2: Diagnostic tree (four branches). Module 3: Content (only after 1 close). Log is the spine. History skips scripts, not logging.
